compiled_state.title: As a future maintainer, I can read the tag policy in the architecture doc
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Document the tag policy — what is guarded, what is not, who writes what, and which limitations are accepted — in `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-system-v3-architecture.md`. Cross-link from the two existing tag contract docs. Add the new CLI commands to the CLAUDE.md Quick Reference.

**Why**: Stories 1–3 ship the mechanism. Without the policy documented, future maintainers will (a) reintroduce full-replacement writes for new tags, (b) re-propose source-enforcement guards that are known infeasible, or (c) try to re-guard `brainstorm:complete` removal (which is intentionally allowed so epics can re-enter brainstorming). The policy doc is what stops the next agent from re-litigating these decisions.

**Scope**:
- IN:
  - New "Tag Policy" section in `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-system-v3-architecture.md` enumerating:
    - Guarded tags — `brainstorm:complete` requires PIL heading at add time (Story 2); explicitly removable (Story 1)
    - Unguarded tags — everything else; policy errs toward flexibility
    - Writer taxonomy — additive `ticket tag`/`untag` (concurrency race accepted, same tolerance as `ticket edit --tags=`) vs the remaining full-replacement `ticket edit --tags=` pattern, which after Story 3 should exist only in the migration script (documented carve-out for provenance)
    - Accepted limitations — concurrent-writer race; source enforcement ("only skill X writes tag Y") infeasible
  - Cross-references added to `plugins/dso/docs/contracts/scrutiny-pending-tag.md` and `plugins/dso/docs/contracts/interaction-deferred-tag.md` linking to the new policy section
  - `CLAUDE.md` Quick Reference table entries for `ticket tag` and `ticket untag`
- OUT: New per-tag contract docs — only the meta-policy layer lives here

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `plugins/dso/docs/ticket-system-v3-architecture.md` contains a "Tag Policy" section listing guarded tags, unguarded tags, writer taxonomy (additive vs full-replacement), and accepted limitations, with an explicit call-out for the migration-script full-replacement carve-out.
  ← Satisfies: "Tag policy (what is guarded, what is not, why convention is load-bearing) is documented in plugins/dso/docs/ticket-system-v3-architecture.md and cross-referenced from docs/contracts/scrutiny-pending-tag.md and docs/contracts/interaction-deferred-tag.md."
- When this story is complete, `scrutiny-pending-tag.md` and `interaction-deferred-tag.md` each contain a cross-reference linking to the new Tag Policy section.
  ← Satisfies: "Tag policy ... is documented in plugins/dso/docs/ticket-system-v3-architecture.md and cross-referenced from docs/contracts/scrutiny-pending-tag.md and docs/contracts/interaction-deferred-tag.md."
- When this story is complete, `CLAUDE.md` Quick Reference table lists `ticket tag` and `ticket untag` with one-line descriptions.
  ← Satisfies: "Tag policy ... is documented in plugins/dso/docs/ticket-system-v3-architecture.md and cross-referenced from docs/contracts/scrutiny-pending-tag.md and docs/contracts/interaction-deferred-tag.md."

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow `.claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md` conventions for formatting, structure, and cross-referencing
- [Documentation] Tag policy must describe ALL tag writers — not just the new CLI. Leaving the full-replacement migration-script carve-out undocumented would misrepresent the system and invite reintroduction of sibling-clobbering patterns.

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for documentation formatting, structure, and conventions.
comments.author: Claude
